notes: Canonical lens catalog for compose macro parsing and skill documentation. Lenses are mental-model cards, not roleplay prompts.

lenses.summary: Treat clear, complete, reader-usable output as the real done condition, then keep iterating until the artifact stands on its own.
lenses.thesis: Non-code work is not finished when text merely exists; it is finished when the intended reader can use it without guesswork, missing context, or silent gaps.
lenses.core_philosophy: Prefer the smallest revision that materially improves clarity, completeness, or accuracy for the reader, and keep looping until the done contract is proven.
mental_model: Read the artifact as the target reader, not as its author.
Treat ambiguity, missing context, and buried assumptions as unfinished work.
Improve the weakest section first instead of rewriting everything.
Use explicit evidence that the artifact is understandable, complete, and current.
decision_rules: Prefer wording that reduces reader uncertainty over wording that sounds impressive.
If a section cannot stand alone for its intended reader, it is not done.
Revise the smallest section that advances the weakest done condition.
Keep looping until the artifact is reader-usable, not just structurally present.
anti_patterns: Text that is technically present but still confusing
Polished wording over missing substance
Stopping after one pass when the weakest condition is still unproven

lenses.summary: Start from the simplest correct mental model, translate jargon into plain words, and add concrete examples before deeper detail.
lenses.thesis: If a general reader cannot follow the core idea in plain language, the explanation is not ready; define the idea simply first, then layer technical detail only as needed.
lenses.core_philosophy: Make the main idea easy without making it wrong, and let the reader climb from simple words to technical precision step by step.
mental_model: Name the main idea in one plain sentence before using technical framing.
Translate unfamiliar terms into everyday language before reintroducing the formal term.
Use one concrete example to anchor the explanation before discussing edge cases.
Add detail only when it changes the reader's understanding or next action.
decision_rules: Prefer plain language over jargon when both are accurate.
Define unavoidable technical terms the first time they appear.
Choose examples that match the reader's likely mental model or workflow.
Keep explanations short enough that a non-expert can retain the thread.
anti_patterns: Dense jargon before first principles
Correct but unreadable expert shorthand
Example-free explanation of an abstract concept

lenses.summary: Data model first, explicit side effects, and explicit performance characteristics.
lenses.thesis: Make the structure and cost of the system visible first, then prefer the simplest explicit mechanism that preserves correctness.
lenses.core_philosophy: Start from the essential user-visible or contract-visible outcome, keep the mechanism explicit, and remove accidental complexity before adding more structure.
mental_model: Name the core outcome and invariant before changing code.
Make data shape, side effects, and cost visible enough to reason about.
Choose the smallest explicit change that satisfies the contract.
Add abstraction only when it removes proven duplication or protects a real invariant.
decision_rules: Model the system in data before proposing structure or abstraction.
Separate transformations from side effects and name the boundary explicitly.
Prefer concrete mechanisms over clever indirection unless the abstraction removes real duplication or sharpens invariants.
Call out allocation, ownership, latency, and complexity characteristics when they matter to the decision.
anti_patterns: Decorative abstraction without a real invariant
Hidden state or hidden side effects
Recommendation without a visible cost model

lenses.summary: Reduce cognitive load, define by exclusions and one dominant constraint, then state the problem plainly.
lenses.thesis: When the question is still foggy, do not chase the perfect prompt first; anchor on a few concrete signals, ask what must not happen, identify the biggest constraint, and turn that into one clear problem statement.
decision_rules: Reduce the input surface to a few concrete anchors before expanding the problem.
Ask what must not happen before asking for the ideal outcome.
Identify one dominant constraint before exploring multiple options.
Return one clear problem statement rather than a menu of fuzzy prompts.
anti_patterns: Broad ideation while the problem is still blurry
Many possible goals with no negative boundary
Treating every constraint as equally important

lenses.summary: Separate observed evidence from inferred risk, expose uncertainty, and resist conclusion-first bias.
lenses.thesis: Good judgement is not strong opinion; it is disciplined separation between what is observed, what is inferred, and what still needs checking.
decision_rules: Separate observed evidence from interpretation before assigning impact.
Mark uncertainty explicitly instead of smoothing it away into confident prose.
Check whether the current conclusion is driven by vivid anecdotes, availability bias, or conclusion-first framing.
Prefer the cheapest discriminating next check when evidence is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Severity inflation without evidence
Confusing likelihood with impact
Treating first-pass intuition as verdict

lenses.summary: Reproduce first, use disprovable hypotheses, and explain the result plainly.
lenses.thesis: If you cannot reproduce the phenomenon, isolate the variables, and explain it in plain language, you do not understand it well enough to trust the fix.
decision_rules: Reduce the failure to the cheapest reproducible case before proposing a fix.
Prefer hypotheses that can be disproved quickly by observation, logs, or tests.
State observed versus expected behavior explicitly before naming root cause.
Leave behind a regression guard when the cause becomes clear.
anti_patterns: Patch-first debugging
Stack-trace worship without a repro path
Cause claims that skip observed-versus-expected framing

lenses.summary: Check explicit contracts against fresh evidence, separate blockers from gaps, and do not claim pass without proof.
lenses.thesis: Final verification is not another review; it is a disciplined check that each explicit contract has evidence, each blocker is isolated, and each missing proof becomes a next check.
decision_rules: Verify every expected contract against concrete evidence before concluding pass.
Separate blocker, evidence gap, and residual polish instead of mixing them in one bucket.
Prefer rerunning the cheapest relevant check over trusting stale success claims.
If a contract cannot be checked fully, mark inconclusive and name the next check.
anti_patterns: Pass claim with no evidence trail
Mixing blocker and polish in the same verdict bucket
Trusting stale success claims without rerun or citation

lenses.summary: Treat release as a sequence of explicit gates and judge only the gate in scope with concrete evidence.
lenses.thesis: Release work becomes unsafe when repository facts, hygiene, readiness, and publication are collapsed into one blurry judgement. Name the gate in scope, verify it directly, and hand off unresolved gates explicitly.
lenses.core_philosophy: Treat release as a chain of explicit gates. Check only the current gate, block on missing proof, and do not borrow confidence from adjacent gates.
mental_model: Name the exact release gate in scope before making any judgement.
Check only gate-specific evidence instead of mixing repository, hygiene, readiness, and publish signals together.
Use blocked or inconclusive when the current gate lacks proof instead of assuming a later gate will absorb the risk.
Hand off the next required release gate explicitly once the current gate is resolved.
decision_rules: Keep repository reality, hygiene, readiness, and publication in separate verdict buckets.
Judge the gate in scope from concrete evidence only.
Escalate missing required proof to blocked or inconclusive instead of optimistic release language.
Record the cheapest next gate or action rather than assuming downstream cleanup.
anti_patterns: Repository, docs, rollout, and publish claims merged into one verdict
Optimistic release language with unresolved gate evidence
Assuming success in one gate because a neighboring gate looks healthy

lenses.summary: Lead with the answer, group supporting points logically, and make scope and evidence hierarchy explicit.
lenses.thesis: A useful contract starts with the answer, then groups supporting points so scope, decision logic, and acceptance boundaries are immediately visible.
decision_rules: State the answer or contract first before details.
Group supporting points into stable buckets instead of chronological narration.
Make in-scope, out-of-scope, and done criteria explicit.
Remove sections that do not change the decision or acceptance boundary.
anti_patterns: Context dump before conclusion
Checklist without grouping logic
Mixed scope and acceptance criteria

lenses.summary: Map competing paradigms, consensus zones, and unresolved anomalies before selecting the next question.
lenses.thesis: Before choosing the best question, first identify the dominant frames, where they agree, and where anomalies remain unresolved.
decision_rules: Identify the major schools of thought before generating evaluative questions.
Separate consensus from unresolved anomalies.
Name the assumptions each camp treats as obvious.
Choose the next question based on the sharpest unresolved disagreement or anomaly.
anti_patterns: Flattening all disagreement into a single axis
Treating consensus as if it were universal
Skipping anomaly mapping before question selection

lenses.summary: Prefer questions that expose testable claims, disconfirming edges, and evidence burdens.
lenses.thesis: A strong question is not merely broad or clever; it creates a surface where claims can fail, evidence can discriminate, and weak answers become obvious.
decision_rules: Rewrite vague prompts into claim-bearing questions with observable failure conditions.
Name the evidence burden required for a strong answer.
Expose disconfirming edges instead of prompting only for confirmation.
Penalize question forms that permit high-fluency but low-testability answers.
anti_patterns: Open-ended prompts with no failure condition
Questions that reward rhetoric over evidence
Forecasts with no explicit claim surface

lenses.summary: Usability-first decisions based on explicit heuristics, scanning behavior, and information scent.
lenses.thesis: Structures become easier to use when navigation, labeling, scanning, and information scent are treated as first-class constraints rather than afterthoughts.
decision_rules: Optimize the first scan path before polishing details.
Check whether the user can predict where to go next from labels, grouping, and information scent.
Prefer predictable headings, navigation, and labels over clever naming.
Treat duplication, orphaned nodes, and hidden paths as structure defects, not just content defects.
anti_patterns: Dense structures with weak labels or headings
Navigation that assumes insider knowledge
Hierarchy that mirrors implementation history instead of user intent

lenses.summary: Systematic risk framing with controls and continuous monitoring lifecycle.
lenses.thesis: Security review should move from assets and boundaries to controls, residual risk, and monitoring, rather than stopping at vulnerability spotting alone.
decision_rules: Frame the system boundary, asset class, and threat-relevant context first.
Map findings to missing, weak, or unverified controls.
Separate immediate mitigation from ongoing monitoring needs.
State residual risk after controls, not only raw issue count.
anti_patterns: Issue lists with no system context
Threat claims without control mapping
Mitigation advice without monitoring or verification

lenses.summary: Small safe iterations with explicit Red-Green-Refactor rhythm.
lenses.thesis: When behavior must stay trustworthy during change, shorten the feedback loop, make the smallest useful check first, and let tests document intent.
decision_rules: Design the smallest test that discriminates the target behavior.
Sequence checks from narrow and cheap to broader and slower.
Prefer tests that protect behavior under change rather than mirror implementation details.
Use explicit behavior buckets: happy path, edge case, failure case.
anti_patterns: Large speculative test plans before identifying the core behavior
Tests coupled to implementation internals
Broad integration checks as the only guard

lenses.summary: Strong boundaries and maintainable architecture with explicit dependency direction.
lenses.thesis: When modules are hard to change safely, first make dependency direction and contract boundaries visible, then remove leaks and forbidden couplings.
decision_rules: State boundary type and dependency direction explicitly.
Check whether policy depends on detail or detail depends on policy.
Treat leaked assumptions across layers as contract defects.
Prefer hardening the boundary before adding new behavior.
anti_patterns: Ambiguous layer ownership
Cross-boundary knowledge leaks
Dependency plans that omit forbidden coupling

lenses.summary: Find the system constraint and optimize end-to-end throughput.
lenses.thesis: Performance work improves when the real bottleneck is identified first and local optimizations are judged by system throughput, not by isolated micro-metrics.
decision_rules: Identify the constraint before proposing optimization work.
Measure throughput or end-to-end effect, not just local speedups.
Explain whether the change exploits, subordinates to, or elevates the constraint.
Reject optimizations that do not move the real bottleneck.
anti_patterns: Optimizing a non-constraint
Benchmarking without a throughput story
Perf work that omits the baseline

lenses.summary: Use seams and staged cutovers for structural change.
lenses.thesis: Structural change is safer when it proceeds through explicit seams, staged replacement, and rollback-friendly cutovers rather than big-bang rewrites.
decision_rules: Identify seams before identifying target architecture steps.
Prefer small reversible cutovers over wide rewrites.
Specify rollback or coexistence strategy alongside each step.
Treat structural isolation as progress, even before feature payoff.
anti_patterns: Big-bang refactor plan
Cutover steps with no rollback path
Refactor goals that mix behavior change with structural isolation

lenses.summary: Reliability engineering with measurable delivery and stability indicators.
lenses.thesis: Release judgement should weigh blast radius, rollback and recovery readiness, and delivery stability signals together rather than treating shipping as a binary ritual.
decision_rules: Check blast radius and rollback readiness before release confidence language.
Use delivery and stability signals together, not in isolation.
Treat missing observability or recovery evidence as release risk.
Prefer no-go with a specific readiness gap over optimistic release language.
anti_patterns: Release decisions with no rollback story
Stability claims with no delivery signal
Go decision justified only by hope or schedule pressure

lenses.summary: Prioritize by urgency and importance while keeping the critical path explicit.
lenses.thesis: A plan becomes execution-ready when priority, dependency, and decision gates are visible enough to prevent busywork from displacing the real critical path.
decision_rules: Separate urgent from important before sequencing tasks.
Expose the critical path and explicit decision gates.
Prefer plans that shorten the path to validated completion rather than maximizing task count.
Push non-critical nice-to-have work out of the main path.
anti_patterns: Flat task lists with no priority logic
Critical dependencies hidden in prose
Plans optimized for completeness instead of execution

lenses.summary: Architecture decisions reviewed across security, reliability, performance, cost, and operations.
lenses.thesis: Cloud architecture decisions improve when tradeoffs are made explicit across pillars instead of optimized for one local concern.
decision_rules: Review the decision against multiple architecture pillars.
State which pillar is being traded off and why.
Prefer remediation priorities tied to the largest cross-pillar risk.
anti_patterns: Single-pillar optimization
Architecture claims with no operations story
Tradeoff-free architecture language

lenses.summary: Define customer progress as job-to-be-done and map competing alternatives.
lenses.thesis: User demand becomes clearer when the desired progress and competing alternatives are framed before solution design.
lenses.core_philosophy: Start from the progress the user is trying to make, then define success, non-goals, and switching pressure before you talk about features.
mental_model: Describe the user's desired progress in plain language before naming any solution.
Name the current workaround or alternative the user is already hiring.
Define what success would look like if the user switched to the new approach.
Keep non-goals explicit so the brief does not quietly become a feature wishlist.
decision_rules: Name the progress the user is hiring the solution to make.
List current alternatives before inventing new features.
Judge proposed work against switching triggers and success criteria.
anti_patterns: Feature language without job framing
Ignoring existing alternatives
Benefit claims with no progress metric

lenses.summary: Human-centered discovery, iterative prototyping, and learning loops.
lenses.thesis: When the problem is still ambiguous, prototype to learn about users rather than trying to reason the whole solution out in prose.
decision_rules: Turn uncertainty into a testable prototype or learning loop.
Start from user pain and behavior before shaping the artifact.
Use prototypes to discover, not to defend a predetermined answer.
anti_patterns: Feature decision before user insight
Prototype as decoration rather than learning tool
One-pass design without feedback

lenses.summary: Reduce conceptual noise and enforce coherent system rules.
lenses.thesis: Design quality rises when the concept is simplified until the primary flow and system rules can be felt without explanation.
decision_rules: State the primary intent in one sentence.
Remove elements that do not support the main flow.
Prefer coherent system rules over one-off flourishes.
anti_patterns: Visual novelty without system logic
Primary flow obscured by decoration
Edge-state handling bolted on late

lenses.summary: Data and evaluation first, then iterate through measurable failure buckets.
lenses.thesis: ML systems improve fastest when the error surface is bucketed and the evaluation loop drives the next change.
decision_rules: Define the eval before changing the model or prompt.
Bucket failures before proposing a global fix.
Prefer interventions that improve measurable failure buckets.
anti_patterns: Model tinkering without eval discipline
Single aggregate metric with no error buckets
Prompt or model changes with no before-and-after evidence

lenses.summary: Hypothesis-driven loops with Build-Measure-Learn and validated learning.
lenses.thesis: When uncertainty is high, ship the smallest experiment that can update the decision, then learn before scaling effort.
decision_rules: Frame the next step as a hypothesis and learning goal.
Measure behavior change, not just activity.
Decide pivot or persevere explicitly after the result.
anti_patterns: Shipping without learning intent
Metrics with no decision consequence
Large build before small experiment

lenses.summary: Bounded appetite, shaping before betting, and clear hill charts.
lenses.thesis: Execution gets sharper when scope is shaped to fit an appetite and ambiguity is reduced before the team starts building.
decision_rules: Set appetite before detailing scope.
Shape the work enough to expose rough edges before execution.
Treat hill-chart progress as uncertainty reduction, not task completion.
anti_patterns: Unbounded scope before execution
Detailed backlog with no appetite
Task progress confused with risk reduction

lenses.summary: Message architecture with Why-How-What and StoryBrand framing.
lenses.thesis: A message becomes clearer when purpose, method, and user-relevant narrative are aligned before detail.
decision_rules: State why before enumerating features.
Map the message to a clear user problem and transformation.
Remove phrases that do not help the audience decide or understand.
anti_patterns: Feature lists with no narrative arc
Purpose and mechanism mixed together
Audience-agnostic messaging

lenses.summary: Map value chain and evolution stage before strategic choices.
lenses.thesis: Good strategy starts with situational awareness: where value is created, what stage each component is in, and which moves fit that terrain.
decision_rules: Map the user need and value chain first.
Place components on the evolution axis before deciding build, buy, or differentiate.
Prefer moves that fit the current landscape rather than abstract best practice.
anti_patterns: Strategy without landscape mapping
Treating all components as equally differentiating
Generic recommendations detached from context

lenses.summary: Public short alias for the default engineering lens.
Public short alias for the debugging lens.
Public short alias for the planning lens.
Public short alias for the UX lens.
Public short alias for the security lens.
